description: Rules to ensure that all development, testing, and deployment environments are identical to prevent environment-specific bugs.
philosophy: There should only be one way to set up and run this project, and it must be reproducible everywhere.

rules.name: Pinned Dependency Versions
rules.description: All project dependencies in `package.json` MUST be pinned to exact versions.
guidelines: Do not use version ranges like `^` (caret) or `~` (tilde).
Use a lockfile (`package-lock.json` or `yarn.lock`) to ensure deterministic installs.
The lockfile MUST be committed to the repository.
rules.purpose: To prevent unexpected breaking changes from minor or patch updates of dependencies.

rules.name: Node.js Version Lock
rules.description: The project MUST specify the exact Node.js version it runs on.
rules.implementation: Create a `.nvmrc` file in the project root containing the required Node.js version (e.g., `v18.18.0`).
rules.guideline: All developers and CI/CD pipelines must use a tool like NVM (Node Version Manager) to switch to the version specified in `.nvmrc`.

rules.name: Environment Variable Schema
rules.description: All required environment variables MUST be documented in a template file.
rules.implementation: Create an `.env.example` file in the project root that lists all required environment variables with placeholder or empty values.
rules.purpose: To provide a single source of truth for the application's configuration and simplify the setup process for new developers.
